title: AEP Conformance Test Case (v0.1)
description: Language-agnostic test case format. Each SDK implements a thin harness (see HARNESS.md) that reads cases of this shape, runs them against its implementation, and asserts. Cases describe (a) the Config the runner receives, (b) a deterministic script of model responses, tool outputs, and supervisor messages, and (c) expectations over the emitted event trajectory. This is the operational answer to spec drift: any behavior the spec mandates should be encoded as a case here, and every SDK MUST pass.

id.description: Stable identifier. SHOULD match the file name (without .json).

title.description: One-line summary of what this case asserts.

description.description: Optional longer prose. Cite the specific spec ambiguity or requirement this case pins down.

spec_refs.description: Section refs into spec/v0.1/SPEC.md or schema fragments this case verifies.

applies_to.description: Which side(s) of the seam this case validates. Most cases test runner behavior given a scripted supervisor; supervisor cases assert the supervisor produces conforming messages given a scripted runner.

scripted_model.description: One entry per turn the harness should serve in order. The harness's mock model returns these responses to the runner regardless of conversation history.

scripted_tools.description: Map from tool name to the response the harness returns when that tool is called. Tools not listed here are assumed to be supervisor-executed (declared in config.tools) and routed via tool_exec_requested.

scripted_supervisor.description: Triggered actions for the harness's mock supervisor. When an event matching 'on' is observed, the harness sends 'send' (or, if 'skip' is true, deliberately does nothing — used to test timeouts).

runner_builtin_tools.description: Optional. Declares the runner's built-in tool catalog so cases can exercise SPEC.md §13.1.2 (agent_started.tools reflects effective surface = Config.tools ∪ runner_builtin_tools, filtered by allowed_tools). Each entry is a tool descriptor the runner exposes natively (e.g., bash, read_file). Cases that don't set this exercise the Config.tools-only path.

events.description: Ordered patterns the trajectory must contain (see 'ordering').

ordering.description: in_order_subsequence: matches must appear in this order, but unmatched events MAY interleave. in_order_strict: matches must be the entire trajectory in order, no extras. any_order: matches must each appear somewhere.

forbidden_events.description: Patterns that MUST NOT appear anywhere in the trajectory.

converged.description: When true and tool_calls is empty, the runner should treat the response as terminal and stop with reason=converged.

skip.description: When true, the harness deliberately does NOT respond — used to assert timeout behavior.

EventMatcher.description: Partial-match pattern. Every key/value in 'match' must appear in the event with that exact value (deep-equal for nested objects). Other keys MAY be present.

label.description: Human-readable name for assertion failure messages.
